blog.title: Featured Content

blog.spotlightTitle: What Is Carbon Fiber? Properties and Application Areas
blog.spotlightBodyPrimary: Carbon fiber is a reinforcement material used in composite structures that require high strength, low weight and dimensional stability. It is widely preferred in aerospace, automotive, defense, industrial equipment and custom machine components where lightweighting and durability must work together.
blog.spotlightBodySecondary: Its theoretical advantages do not translate into field performance unless resin choice, layup structure, finish expectation and production method are aligned correctly. In practice, carbon fiber is not only a material choice but also an application and process decision.
blog.insightText: Technical scope, application impact and the checkpoints that shape a sound decision

items.one: We review your request and clarify technical scope, operating environment, quantity and critical performance expectations.
items.two: If needed, we request additional details around material, process, tolerance, drawings or sampling needs.
items.three: We then share the right product, sample or quotation path together with a clear lead-time expectation.

homeTeaser.title: Full project form
homeTeaser.body: Attach drawings, specifications and quantity expectations on the contact page. Our team routes technical review and quotation from there.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.intro: Short answers about our B2B composite manufacturing and quotation workflow.
faq.q1: Is there a minimum order quantity (MOQ)?
faq.a1: MOQ depends on part geometry, tooling needs, material and process type. Share drawings or specifications via the contact form and we will assess your project specifically.
faq.q2: How long does sample production take?
faq.a2: Lead time varies with complexity, finish expectations and existing tooling. After receiving your request we share an indicative timeline and next steps.
faq.q3: Which materials do you work with?
faq.a3: We support carbon fiber, FRP/GRP, fiberglass and hybrid reinforcement structures for panels, profiles, enclosures and custom industrial parts.
faq.q4: Which technical drawing formats do you accept?
faq.a4: STEP, IGES, DXF/DWG and PDF specifications are commonly used. You can attach files through the quote or contact flow.
faq.q5: Do you support export shipping and logistics?
faq.a5: B2B shipments can include export-oriented packaging and documentation; details are aligned per project.

offer.title: Quote Request
offer.description: Let us prepare a project-specific quote, process recommendation and production approach based on your material type, dimensions, quantity, target delivery and application conditions.

benefits.title: What We Clarify in the Quote

items.one: Material selection: carbon fiber, FRP, fiberglass or hybrid solutions
items.two: Manufacturing route: molded part, panel, profile, tube or custom component process
items.three: Quantity, lead time, finish expectations, quality and logistics planning

one.title: Engineering Support
one.body: We clarify your technical requirements together — from material selection and production route to tolerances and surface quality.

two.title: Rapid Prototyping
two.body: We complete the sample and prototype phase quickly, providing an iterative process open to revisions.

three.title: Series Production
three.body: From an approved prototype to consistent series production, we establish a scalable process structure.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How long does the quote process take?
faq.a1: Depending on the project details, we typically complete our technical review and deliver a preliminary quote within 1–3 business days.
faq.q2: Can I order in small quantities?
faq.a2: Yes. We accommodate samples, prototypes and small-batch orders; minimum quantities are evaluated on a per-project basis.
faq.q3: Can I get a quote without technical drawings?
faq.a3: Yes. We can start with dimensions, operating conditions and quantity information. We can also support you during the technical drawing process if needed.

ensotek.label: Ensotek Link
ensotek.title: Composite manufacturing backed by Ensotek engineering
ensotek.description: MOE Kompozit is Ensotek's composite sub-brand, built on industrial production, quality management and project discipline. Ensotek's CTP/FRP material know-how from the cooling tower sector is transferred into carbon fiber, fiberglass, custom panels, covers, housings and project-specific composite parts.
